meaning: n.姿势,姿态v.(使...)摆好姿势,形成,引起,造成vi.摆姿势,佯装,矫揉造作
examples: She rearranged herself in another pose.
They pose a serious threat to security.
He adopted a relaxed pose for the camera.
Audrey affects an insouciant pose and asks me to take a shot on her phone.
It talked about it being "housed in buildings which pose intolerable risks to people and the environment".
